  • Please also make sure that the phone numbers are clickable.
California Wrongful Death Law Overview
Losing a loved one to someone else’s negligence is one of the hardest things a family can endure. In Sacramento, wrongful death laws give surviving relatives a path to seek accountability and financial support when a preventable tragedy occurs.

A wrongful death claim in California is a civil action brought when a person’s death is caused by the wrongful act or negligence of another. In Sacramento, these cases frequently stem from incidents such as car accidents on I-5 or Highway 99 (/car-accidents/), motorcycle crashes on busy roads like J Street or Folsom Boulevard (/motorcycle-accidents/), truck collisions on I-80 (/truck-accidents/), dangerous property conditions, defective products, or medical malpractice at local hospitals such as UC Davis Medical Center, Sutter General, or Mercy San Juan.

Who Can File a Wrongful Death Claim in California?
Under California law (Code of Civil Procedure §377.60), the following people are typically allowed to bring a wrongful death lawsuit:
    •    The spouse or domestic partner of the deceased
    •    The children of the deceased
    •    Stepchildren or dependent minors living in the household
    •    If none of the above survive, other relatives who would inherit under California’s intestate succession laws

Types of Damages Available
Families may be able to recover compensation for both economic and non-economic losses, such as:
    •    Funeral and burial expenses in Sacramento or surrounding counties
    •    Loss of the deceased’s financial support and income
    •    The value of household services the loved one would have provided
    •    Loss of love, companionship, guidance, and emotional support

Statute of Limitations
Most wrongful death lawsuits in California must be filed within two years from the date of death. Families in Sacramento should not wait to take action — missing this deadline can prevent you from pursuing justice. A knowledgeable Sacramento wrongful death lawyer (/wrongful-death/) can make sure your claim is filed properly and on time.

Why Local Legal Help Matters
Wrongful death cases often involve major insurance carriers and defense attorneys who try to minimize payouts. Having an experienced Sacramento attorney who knows the local courts, judges, and insurance tactics can make a powerful difference in protecting your family’s future.

⸻

We’re Here for Your Family
If your family has lost a loved one because of negligence, we are here to help you find answers and pursue justice. Call us today at (916) 921-6400 or (800) 404-5400 for a free, compassionate consultation with a Sacramento wrongful death attorney.

description:

From the client:
FAQs About Who Can File a Wrongful Death Claim in California

Can siblings bring a wrongful death claim in California?
In most cases, siblings do not have standing to file unless they were financially dependent on the person who passed away. 

Priority is given to spouses, children, and parents.

What if the deceased was unmarried with no children?
If there is no surviving spouse or child, the parents may bring the claim. If parents are no longer living, other dependents such as stepchildren or financially supported relatives may have rights.

Can multiple family members file separate claims?
No. California law requires all eligible heirs to join together in a single wrongful death action. This ensures fairness and prevents multiple lawsuits over the same loss.

What if we’re unsure who can file in our family?
Every situation is unique. An experienced wrongful death lawyer can review your family’s circumstances and explain who has the legal right to bring a claim.

Beyond the grief, there are often unanswered questions, financial strain, and the feeling that life will never be the same. While no legal action can replace what was lost, a wrongful death claim can bring accountability and provide the resources a family needs to begin rebuilding.

For more than 40 years, our Sacramento wrongful death lawyers have stood beside families facing these painful circumstances. We understand the emotional weight of these cases and approach them with compassion, respect, and the determination to seek justice. Whether the loss was caused by a car crash, medical negligence, or another act of carelessness, we are here to protect your rights and fight for the support your family deserves.


When you’re ready to talk, we’re here to help. Call us at (916) 921-6400.


A Sacramento Family’s Story
After a tragic crash on Highway 99 took the life of a young father, his family was left grieving and unsure how to manage mounting bills. The insurance company offered a quick settlement that would not have covered even a fraction of their long-term needs. Our wrongful death team stepped in, gathered evidence, and worked with experts to fully value the family’s loss — from lost income to the irreplaceable role of a husband and parent. In the end, we secured a settlement that provided financial stability and, just as importantly, a sense of justice.

Thanks much

title: 10DLC Discussion
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag
description:
Dear Clixsy:

We're trying to work with our phone provider GoTo to get ourselves compliant with the 10DLC requirements for continuing to use SMS text messaging with our current and potential clients, specifically those who submit their phone numbers via the contact form on our site at autoaccident.com.

Are you familiar with the requirements for 10DLC compliance?  (GoTo does not make it a straightforward experience.)  They indicate that we will need the following:

The "How Can We Help" form on our website's contact page and a couple other pages needs:
  • A second check box to explicitly opt out of receiving text messages.
  • The reference in the form to "Terms" should read "Terms of Service" and should include the link to our page at https://autoaccident.com/terms-of-service/
  • The reference in the form to "Privacy Policy" should include the link to our page at https://autoaccident.com/privacy-policy/

I.e., the text "Terms of Service" and "Privacy Policy" need to specifically be within the body of the form itself, not just included lower down on the page.  That text currently in the form is not linked.

Please advise when these changes can be completed to that we can re-submit our site for approval.  We understand the deadline for this is mid-January.

Please let me know if you have any questions.  Thank you!

title: Update outdated info on this page
description:
, can you update the copy on this page for the Bodily and Property sections as legislation was updated in January when they passed the Senate Bill 1107, known as the Protect California Drivers Act.

The client didn't have a specific direction on how to update it besides wanting us to reword the policy limits with the new legislation.

Here's some copy that Ed Smith (firm owner) sent over but Jeanette (CMO) is unsure if he created this in perplexity, so please use it as a reference only:

"If personal injury damages exceed the minimum liability insurance coverage required by California law, this may be an issue. The current minimum auto liability limits required in California include:

  • $15,000 per person for serious injury or wrongful death
  • $30,000 for all parties involved in the traffic accident
  • $5,000 property damage
However, at the beginning of 2025, California drivers will face higher minimum auto insurance liability limits due to Senate Bill 1107, also known as the Protect California Drivers Act. This legislation raises the required coverage to $30,000 for bodily injury or death per person, $60,000 per accident, and $15,000 for property damage. By 2035, these amounts will increase further, ensuring even more comprehensive coverage.

For you as a driver, these changes mean better financial protection if you’re involved in an accident, reducing the chance of paying for damages or injuries yourself. However, with higher coverage requirements, you can expect your auto insurance premiums to increase as insurance companies adjust their rates. While this might raise your monthly payments, the added protection could be crucial in serious accidents, giving you greater security and peace of mind while driving."


I highlighted the areas that are outdated according to what they sent over. Let me know if you need anything else from me.

title: Reporting Call Prep
description:
This task must be fully updated by EOD Monday before the reporting call.

This document serves as:
  • Internal alignment between AM, CM, and SEO
  • Source of truth for call structure
  • Risk detection tool
  • Talking point preparation
No one should walk into the reporting call unprepared.

This prep aligns directly with the live call agenda.

AM should use this document to guide the call in real time.

No one should enter the call without this fully filled out.

SECTION 1: OPENING FRAME (AM)


What tone are we setting?

Client temperature this month:
 ☐ Strong
 ☐ Neutral
 ☐ Slightly concerned
 ☐ Frustrated

Notes from weekly pulse:


Any relationship items to acknowledge at opening?

Opening focus reminder:
What do we want the client to feel by minute 3?

SECTION 2: PERFORMANCE OVERVIEW (CM SECTION)


@LeadSEO please provide by Wednesday 12 PM:
  1. How is performance this month?
  2. Campaign health status: Green / Yellow / Red
  3. Areas of concern
  4. Anything stand out? Wins or things we need to be aware of?
  5. Do you need anything from the client?
  6. What is our main focus next month?
AM must review and confirm alignment with CM before call.

Risk Flag:
 ☐ No performance concerns
 ☐ Minor fluctuation
 ☐ Underperformance risk

If risk exists:
  • Has CM been aligned?
  • Has AM Lead been notified?
No surprises allowed on call.

SECTION 3: PERFORMANCE Q&A PREP


List anticipated performance questions:




Confirm:

☐ CM prepared for these
☐ Messaging aligned
☐ Time boundary plan set

SECTION 4: WORK COMPLETED (AM SECTION)


This mirrors the “Campaign Execution” portion of the call.

List in client-friendly language:
  • SEO deliverables completed:

  • Content published:

  • Links built:

  • Technical fixes:

  • Dev completed:

  • Wins (Sentinel, visibility gains, authority growth):

No internal jargon.

SECTION 5: WORK IN PROGRESS (AM)


  • Active SEO initiatives:

  • Content pipeline:

  • Technical initiatives:

  • Dev in progress:

  • Strategic pushes:

Must clearly show momentum.

SECTION 6: TEAM NEEDS (AM)


Do we need anything from the client?
  • Approvals:

  • Content:

  • Access:

  • Decisions:


If nothing needed, state clearly: None at this time.

SECTION 7: MONTH AHEAD VISION (AM)


This section drives confidence.

Answer clearly:
  • What is our main focus next month?
  • What are we monitoring closely?
  • What would success look like by next reporting call?

SECTION 8: FINAL Q&A PREP (AM)


Non-performance questions the client may raise:


Plan response or follow-up pathway.

Confirm:

☐ If unsure, defer and follow up
☐ CM will not re-enter unless pre-discussed

SECTION 9: ESCALATION CHECK


Before call, confirm:

☐ SEO updates received
☐ Dev updates confirmed
☐ Any blockers escalated
☐ AM Lead notified if risk exists
☐ CM aligned on any sensitive topics

If any box cannot be checked, escalate before call.

SECTION 10: CALL CONTROL REMINDERS (AM)


  • Set agenda at beginning
  • Protect time at minute 20
  • Transition clearly
  • Do not guess
  • Close confidently

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline w/ a group of tasks we have from a sheet. Here is the link for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:

Navigating Wildfire Losses in California

The increasing frequency and intensity of California wildfires have left many residents facing devastating losses. If you’ve experienced significant damage or lost everything to a wildfire, navigating the insurance claim process can feel overwhelming. Many insurance companies may deny or underpay claims, citing policy loopholes or fine print.

At AutoAccident.com, we specialize in helping wildfire victims recover the compensation they deserve. Whether you’re battling your insurer or seeking accountability from negligent parties like utility companies, our experienced legal team is here to fight for you. From handling complex insurance claims and negotiating settlements to representing you in court, we provide comprehensive legal support to help you rebuild your life.

With over four decades of experience, we’ve stood by countless California families in their toughest times. Together, we’ll help you turn a devastating loss into a story of resilience and recovery.

You’re not alone. Let’s take the first step forward today.

You're Invited: Join Us for Lunch and Learn About How to properly figure out the value of your personal injury case

 
Have you or a loved one been injured in an accident?
 
Are you feeling overwhelmed by medical bills, insurance paperwork, and legal jargon?
 
We understand how confusing and stressful this time can be.
 
That's why we're extending a warm invitation to join us for a free, informative luncheon designed to help you understand how a personal injury case is properly valued.
 
 
What's This All About?
 
At AutoAccident.com, we believe that knowledge is power. We've seen firsthand how a little bit of legal education can make a world of difference for accident victims and their families.
 
That's why we're hosting a series of free luncheons where we share valuable insights about personal injury law in a relaxed, pressure-free environment.
 
 
Why Should You Attend?
 
Get Informed, Not Pressured.
 
This isn't a sales pitch. It's an opportunity to learn about your rights and options without any obligation.
 
 
Expert Knowledge, Simplified
 
We'll break down complex legal concepts into easy-to-understand information you can actually use.
 
 
Ask Questions, Get Answers
 
Bring your burning questions about personal injury law, and we'll provide clear, honest answers.
 

Enjoy a Delicious Meal

Meet Others in Similar Situations
 
Connect with fellow community members who understand what you're going through.
 

What Will You Learn?

Who Should Attend?
 
Our luncheons are perfect for:
 
- Individuals who have recently been injured in an accident

- Anyone interested in learning more about personal injury law in California
 
 
When and Where?

 "In the realm of personal injury law, it is crucial for clients to be wary of attorneys who promise a specific case value upfront. Such assurances are inherently misleading because the true worth of a personal injury case cannot be accurately determined until the claimant has completed medical treatment and all injuries have been thoroughly documented and reviewed in medical records. This comprehensive evaluation is essential to understanding the full extent of the injuries and their long-term impact on the individual's life. Any attorney who guarantees a certain settlement amount before this process is complete is providing misinformation. These premature promises are not only false but can also set unrealistic expectations, potentially leading to disappointment and mistrust. Clients should seek legal counsel that emphasizes a thorough and honest assessment of their case, based on concrete medical evidence and a careful analysis of all relevant factors."

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466
